body.title: One queryable table for every LLM call system-wide (Willison)
body.result: m054_llm_calls migration + _write_llm_call_db() + dual-write in log_inference_io() and IgorBase.log_llm_io(); 5 tests green
body.description:
Simon Willison's observability-first principle: every LLM call should be logged with provenance and queryable without reading log files. Currently pe_chain writes inference calls to reasoning_calls.log (file-based). NE turns, habit evaluations, and palace reads that call LLMs have no unified audit trail. Create instance.llm_calls table: id, ts, prompt_hash (md5 of prompt), model, tokens_in, tokens_out, outcome (pass/fail/unknown), source_fn (caller), ticket_id (nullable), elapsed_ms. Wire IgorBase.log_llm_io() to write there (it currently writes JSON to daily files — keep file write, ADD DB write). Wire pe_chain _log_pe_inference() to write there too.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/igor_base.py (log_llm_io), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (_log_pe_inference), migration SQL for instance.llm_calls
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/database (safe migration), theigors/rules/docs-live-in-code
**Scope boundary:** In scope: schema + wiring IgorBase + pe_chain to write there. Out of scope: NE turn logging (separate ticket), UI to query it.
**Test plan:** After a pe_chain hypothesize call, instance.llm_calls has a row with correct prompt_hash and model. After IgorBase.log_llm_io(), same table has a row. File log still works (dual-write, don't break existing).
